steps.title: Identify the cost components
steps.content:
Three components make up the total cost:
  • First hour: flat $195
  • Additional hours: $75 per hour beyond the first
  • Gas: $w$
Since $x$ is total hours rented, the number of additional hours is $(x - 1)$.

steps.title: Write the equation
steps.content:
$$y = 195 + 75(x - 1) + w$$
Verify with $x = 1$: $y = 195 + 75(0) + w = 195 + w$. Correct — only the flat first-hour fee and gas. Verify with $x = 3$: $y = 195 + 75(2) + w = 345 + w$. This is $195 for hr 1$ + $75 each for hrs 2 and 3$ + gas. Correct.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$y = 195 + 75(x-1) + w$': Correctly charges $195 flat for the first hour and $75 for each of the $(x-1)$ additional hours. ✓
  • Choice B — '$y = 195 + 75x + w$': Charges $75x$ instead of $75(x-1)$, which would charge $75 for the first hour on top of the $195 flat fee — overcounting. ✗
  • Choice C — '$y = 195 + 75 + w$': A constant — doesn't depend on $x$ at all. Clearly wrong for varying rental lengths. ✗
  • Choice D — '$y = 75(x-1) + w$': Omits the $195 first-hour charge entirely. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.

explanation:
• Choice A — '$y = 195 + 75(x-1) + w$': $195 flat for hour 1, plus $75 for each of $(x-1)$ additional hours, plus gas. At $x=1$: $y = 195 + w$. ✓
  • Choice B — '$y = 195 + 75x + w$': Charges $75 for all $x$ hours including the first, double-billing the first hour on top of the $195. ✗
  • Choice C — '$y = 195 + 75 + w$': Constant; ignores $x$ entirely. ✗
  • Choice D — '$y = 75(x-1) + w$': Forgets the $195 first-hour flat charge. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
